pattern_candidates.title: Skill install with version stamping
pattern_candidates.what: A ~280-line CLI module that reads SKILL.md from package data, stamps the CLI version as an HTML comment in the frontmatter, and writes it to ~/.claude/skills/<name>/ and ~/.agents/skills/<name>/ (or project-scoped). Plus status and uninstall subcommands with version drift detection.
pattern_candidates.why_novel: Solves cross-locale skill drift. You currently have no distinction between canonical source and installed copy — everything is a single file in .claude/skills/. This pattern introduces that distinction cleanly with minimal code.
pattern_candidates.effort: E2 — port Python logic to Node, replace hatchling force-include with a build step or git reference, design a canonical source location
pattern_candidates.relevance: Very high — directly addresses cross_locale_config memory and unblocks JASON-OS portability
pattern_candidates.novelty: High — you don't have this pattern today

pattern_candidates.title: Autonomy Rules section in SKILL.md
pattern_candidates.what: A structured section in the skill definition that splits every command into 'Run automatically (no confirmation)' vs 'Ask before running', with a one-line reason for each.
pattern_candidates.why_novel: Your skills encode autonomy via project-level CLAUDE.md guardrails and per-agent tool permissions. Per-skill per-command autonomy contracts are missing. This gives agents a structured lookup table instead of inferring from prose.
pattern_candidates.effort: E1 — add to existing high-risk skills, no infrastructure change
pattern_candidates.relevance: High — directly applicable to add-debt, pr-review, session-end, audit-* skills
pattern_candidates.novelty: Medium — you have the concept, not the structure

pattern_candidates.title: Nightly RPC health check with auto-filed issues
pattern_candidates.what: Nightly workflow that verifies each of 35+ RPC method IDs still round-trips with the server. On mismatch, auto-creates a GitHub issue with bug/rpc-breakage/automated labels showing expected vs actual IDs.
pattern_candidates.why_novel: Pattern for health-checking dependencies you don't control. Doesn't apply to Firebase (which has stable contracts) but could apply to: Claude Code tool API contract, Anthropic SDK version drift, external MCP server health, GitHub Actions API changes.
pattern_candidates.effort: E2 — adapt to a specific external contract you care about
pattern_candidates.relevance: Medium — latent, activates when you identify an external contract to monitor

pattern_candidates.title: PyPI README link substitution at build time
pattern_candidates.what: Build-time regex substitutions that rewrite relative doc links in README.md to version-tagged absolute GitHub URLs. Result: PyPI README at v0.3.4 always links to v0.3.4 docs, not main.
pattern_candidates.why_novel: Solves the 'broken links on PyPI README' problem and the 'stale version in docs' problem simultaneously. Latent value for you — relevant when you eventually publish anything.
pattern_candidates.relevance: Low today, medium if you publish

pattern_candidates.title: Ambient CLAUDE.md with mandatory PR workflow
pattern_candidates.what: Project CLAUDE.md embeds a mandatory 4-step PR workflow (monitor CI → check review comments → address each → reply with commit SHA → verify CLEAN) that ANY Claude Code session in the repo picks up automatically.
pattern_candidates.why_novel: Contrasts with your invoked /pr-review skill. Design question: ambient (always loaded) vs invoked (on demand) agent guidance. They answer 'critical process discipline goes ambient'. Your CLAUDE.md today has guardrails but not the step-by-step process embedded.
pattern_candidates.effort: E1 — 30 minute edit to your own CLAUDE.md to embed highest-criticality workflows

pattern_candidates.title: Embedded Task() subagent patterns in SKILL.md workflows
source_files: SKILL.md ('Common Workflows' section, e.g. 'Research to Podcast (Automated with Subagent)')
pattern_candidates.what: Workflow sections in SKILL.md include literal Task(prompt=..., subagent_type=...) invocations that agents can copy-paste. Treats subagent spawning as a normal workflow primitive, not an escape hatch.
pattern_candidates.why_novel: Your skills reference agents abstractly ('spawn a research agent'); theirs provide the exact invocation. Reduces agent cognitive load from 'design the task prompt' to 'fill in the parameters'.
pattern_candidates.effort: E1 — retrofit long-running workflows in existing skills
pattern_candidates.relevance: Medium-high for skills with async/long-running operations

knowledge_candidates.title: Skill-as-pip-package distribution model
knowledge_candidates.what_to_learn: How to ship a skill as installable software: canonical source vs installed copy, version stamping, multi-target install, drift detection.

knowledge_candidates.title: SKILL.md as executable spec (full structure study)
knowledge_candidates.what_to_learn: A 500-line template for structuring agent skills: activation triggers in frontmatter → prerequisites → parallel-agent safety → autonomy rules → quick reference → output format schemas → workflow with subagent patterns → error decision trees → exit code contracts.

knowledge_candidates.title: Engineering discipline against undocumented APIs
knowledge_candidates.what_to_learn: How to operate robustly when you depend on an interface you don't control: single source of truth, nightly round-trip verification, user self-recovery docs, explicit stability contracts.

knowledge_candidates.title: Build-time README link templating
knowledge_candidates.what_to_learn: Hatchling fancy-pypi-readme substitutions. How to solve the 'README on package index has broken relative links' problem with regex substitutions.

knowledge_candidates.title: Ambient vs invoked agent guidance design
knowledge_candidates.what_to_learn: When should a workflow be embedded in CLAUDE.md (ambient, always-loaded) vs an invoked skill? Their answer: high-criticality process discipline goes ambient. Worth comparing with your approach.

knowledge_candidates.title: Three-tier test strategy with VCR
knowledge_candidates.what_to_learn: Unit (pure) / integration (VCR-recorded HTTP) / e2e (real API, pytest-marked) with cassette recording discipline.
knowledge_candidates.active_project: Conceptual — your test discipline is different but shares goals

knowledge_candidates.title: Click command organization at scale
knowledge_candidates.what_to_learn: How to decompose a ~50-command CLI into named groups without losing discoverability.

knowledge_candidates.title: SourceFulltext.find_citation_context pattern
knowledge_candidates.what_to_learn: API ergonomics when the underlying data is messy — helper that locates citation snippets with position tracking and ambiguity handling.

knowledge_candidates.title: Open skills ecosystem (npx skills add ...)
knowledge_candidates.what_to_learn: Whether 'npx skills add teng-lin/notebooklm-py' references a real skills registry/distribution ecosystem, and if so, how it works. Potentially highly relevant to JASON-OS distribution.
knowledge_candidates.flag: unverified — determine if real or aspirational
